Echinacea lappifolia is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is commonly known as the white coneflower or green coneflower. It is native to the prairies and open woods of the south-central United States and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea lappifolia include alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and glycosides, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Echinacea lappifolia is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, to support immune function and reduce the severity and duration of upper respiratory tract infections.
